channel.title: Reflection on ftvkyo
channel.description: Recent content in Reflection on ftvkyo

item.title: On self-organization, part 2: But how?
item.description: Reintroduction Purpose Note vault Note vault sections Section one: Journal Wiki Writing Conclusion: the full picture Extras “Steamroller” Dedicated time To-do lists, choices and priorities Bye Reintroduction In the previous part I have gone through some tools and approaches to self-organization. The main focus was on note-taking, and now it is finally time to highlight some neat parts of it. I have already more-or-less described the criteria that I want the note-taking system to satisfy, and why I need it, so here I’m going to talk about its implementation details.

item.title: On self-organization, part 1: But why?
item.description: Introduction So what is the problem, exactly? What strategies do I use? Adjusting the environment Managing distractions Email inbox Structuring time Adjusting myself Note-taking What do I want from note-taking then? Formalization of the note-taking framework Afterword Introduction The purpose of this series of posts is to share some details of my journey of organizing myself. I think this might be interesting or even useful for somebody, but I am mostly writing this to stay motivated to continue (and as writing practice).
